**Elaine Giftos**[[1]](#cite_note-Age17AtJune1960-1) is an American retired model, actress, and dancer.

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Elaine_Giftos&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

The daughter of Mr. and Mrs. Charles P. Giftos,[[1]](#cite_note-Age17AtJune1960-1) she attended [Pittsfield High School](/wiki/Pittsfield_High_School_(Massachusetts) "Pittsfield High School (Massachusetts)").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

While working as a fashion model in New York, Giftos was selected by the [Clairol](/wiki/Clairol "Clairol") company as Miss Ultra Smooth in 1964. In this role, she traveled around the United States promoting the company's soothing lotion for shaving women's legs.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Her work as a photographer's model resulted in photographs of her being used in *Ingenue*, *[Redbook](/wiki/Redbook "Redbook")*, and *[Seventeen](/wiki/Seventeen_(American_magazine) "Seventeen (American magazine)")* magazines.[[4]](#cite_note-slpd-4)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Elaine_Giftos&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Trained by [George Balanchine](/wiki/George_Balanchine "George Balanchine") as a member of the [New York City Ballet](/wiki/New_York_City_Ballet "New York City Ballet"), Giftos performed on Broadway in *Leonard Sillman's New Faces of 1968* and *Pousse-Café*,[[5]](#cite_note-5) then moved to California to pursue a career in movies and television.

Giftos' first television appearance was in an episode of *[I Dream of Jeannie](/wiki/I_Dream_of_Jeannie "I Dream of Jeannie")* ("Jeannie the Matchmaker") as a dating-service clerk. Other shows in which she appeared were *[Bonanza](/wiki/Bonanza "Bonanza")*; *[My Three Sons](/wiki/My_Three_Sons "My Three Sons")*; *[The Interns](/wiki/The_Interns_(television_series) "The Interns (television series)")*; *[Ironside](/wiki/Ironside_(1967_TV_series) "Ironside (1967 TV series)")*; *[Adam-12](/wiki/Adam-12 "Adam-12")*; *[Streets of San Francisco](/wiki/The_Streets_of_San_Francisco "The Streets of San Francisco")*; *[The Paul Lynde Show](/wiki/The_Paul_Lynde_Show "The Paul Lynde Show")*; *[Love, American Style](/wiki/Love,_American_Style "Love, American Style")*; *[The Partridge Family](/wiki/The_Partridge_Family "The Partridge Family")* in a recurring role as Reuben Kinkcaid's girlfriend Bonnie Kleinschmidt; *[Kolchak: The Night Stalker](/wiki/Kolchak:_The_Night_Stalker "Kolchak: The Night Stalker")*; *[The Bob Newhart Show](/wiki/The_Bob_Newhart_Show "The Bob Newhart Show")*; *[The Six Million Dollar Man](/wiki/The_Six_Million_Dollar_Man "The Six Million Dollar Man")*; *[Otherworld](/wiki/Otherworld_(TV_series) "Otherworld (TV series)")*; *[Barney Miller](/wiki/Barney_Miller "Barney Miller")*; *[Three's Company](/wiki/Three%27s_Company "Three's Company")*; *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*; *[Quincy, M.E.](/wiki/Quincy,_M.E. "Quincy, M.E.")*; *[Knight Rider](/wiki/Knight_Rider_(1982_TV_series) "Knight Rider (1982 TV series)")*; *[Magnum, P.I.](/wiki/Magnum,_P.I. "Magnum, P.I.")*; *[Murder, She Wrote](/wiki/Murder,_She_Wrote "Murder, She Wrote")*; *[Tales of the Unexpected](/wiki/Tales_of_the_Unexpected_(TV_series) "Tales of the Unexpected (TV series)")*; *[War of the Worlds](/wiki/War_of_the_Worlds_(1988_TV_series) "War of the Worlds (1988 TV series)")*; *[Ally McBeal](/wiki/Ally_McBeal "Ally McBeal")*; *[Mars and Beyond](/wiki/Mars_and_Beyond "Mars and Beyond")* and *[Family Law](/wiki/Family_Law_(American_TV_series) "Family Law (American TV series)")*.[[6]](#cite_note-TVGuide-6)

Her movie credits include roles in *[On a Clear Day You Can See Forever](/wiki/On_a_Clear_Day_You_Can_See_Forever_(film) "On a Clear Day You Can See Forever (film)")* (1970); *[The Student Nurses](/wiki/The_Student_Nurses "The Student Nurses")* (1970);[[6]](#cite_note-TVGuide-6) *[Gas-s-s-s](/wiki/Gas-s-s-s "Gas-s-s-s")* (1970, as the female lead Cilla);[[7]](#cite_note-7) *[No Drums, No Bugles](/wiki/No_Drums,_No_Bugles "No Drums, No Bugles")* (1972); *[Everything You Always Wanted to Know About Sex\* (\*But Were Afraid to Ask)](/wiki/Everything_You_Always_Wanted_to_Know_About_Sex*_(*But_Were_Afraid_to_Ask)_(film) "Everything You Always Wanted to Know About Sex* (*But Were Afraid to Ask) (film)")* (1972); *[The Wrestler](/wiki/The_Wrestler_(1974_film) "The Wrestler (1974 film)")* (1974); *[Paternity](/wiki/Paternity_(film) "Paternity (film)")* (1981); *[Angel](/wiki/Angel_(1984_film) "Angel (1984 film)")* (1984); and *The Trouble with Dick* (1987).

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Elaine_Giftos&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Giftos was married to writer/producer [Herbert Wright](/wiki/Herbert_Wright_(producer) "Herbert Wright (producer)") until his death in 2005.

Since 1989, Giftos Wright has provided consulting services for [feng shui](/wiki/Feng_shui "Feng shui") under the name The Wright Way of Feng Shui.[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)
